Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases Lets analyze the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observations All cases start with RA then VF then AE After AE there is a possible loop In case 1 we have RMI then VF then AD In case 2 directly AD after AE In case 3 we have RMI RMI then VF then AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the pattern after AE we may have a loop each iteration consists of RMI then VF But in case 3 we have two RMI then one VF That suggests that the loop might be on RMI only and then a single VF follows before AD

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So it seems that after AE we can have a sequence of RMI activities and then a VF then AD But case 2 has zero RMI then directly AD So the extra VF is only present if there is at least one RMI

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we can model the section after AE as XOR of a branch that does loop of RMI then VF and a silent branch tau Then after that we do AD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That yields either RMI plus then VF then AD or AD directly That matches Case 2 AE then choose tau then AD Case 1 one RMI then VF then AD Case 3 two RMI then VF then AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now the overall sequence RA VF AE the XOR AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Our model gives exactly that The model fits This model seems simple and captures the behavior
